results.title: Vo Nguyen
highlights:
Data Scientist at AT&T | AI Developer | Machine Learning Specialist | Passionate Educator
...
Hello! Welcome to my LinkedIn profile—so glad you have a chance to read this! My name is Vo Nguyen, and I'm a Data Scientist at AT&T specializing in Generative AI. I hold a Master's degree in Data Science with a focus on Machine Learning from Southern Methodist University, and I earned my Bachelor's degree in Mathematics (Actuarial Option) from the University of Texas at Austin (Hook 'Em Horns!). As a first-generation American and the first in my family to earn both a college and master's degree, I was born and raised in inner-city Dallas. Thanks to the full-ride Longhorn Opportunity Scholarship, I was able to attend the University of Texas, which set the foundation for my academic and professional journey. I began my career in the insurance industry at New York Life, and then spent eight years as an educator in underprivileged Title 1 schools. During this time, I implemented data-oriented teaching methods to help students succeed, significantly boosting school performance metrics and making a positive impact on students from similar backgrounds. This dedication led me to earn top marks at my school and become a Distinguished Educator in the Dallas Independent School District. My passion for data and its transformative potential drove me to pursue my Master's in Data Science. This path led me to my current role at AT&T, where I work on cutting-edge technology to develop Generative AI solutions for large-scale knowledge management platforms. I'm also helping to create other solutions for RFPs. By utilizing Databricks and advanced machine learning models, our team has significantly optimized workflow efficiency. I enjoy translating complex data findings into actionable insights for stakeholders at all levels, fostering innovation and strategic development within the field of data science. I thrive on thinking critically and quantitatively, embracing challenges, and solving complex problems in team-based environments. My dedication to my craft is fueled by a profound belief in the transformative power of education and technology. Feel free to connect or reach out if you'd like to discuss data science, education, or collaborative opportunities!

### AI Machine Learning Engineer at [AT&T](https://www.linkedin.com/company/att) (Current)
...
Developed real-time AI and machine learning models to monitor network anomalies, predict service outages, and proactively optimize telecom infrastructure using large-scale streaming data from distributed network systems. Applied advanced deep learning and time-series forecasting techniques using TensorFlow and PyTorch to improve capacity planning, traffic forecasting, and spectrum utilization efficiency across telecom networks. Designed and fine-tuned domain-specific NLP pipelines for multilingual customer interactions, enabling accurate intent recognition and automated issue resolution across digital support channels. Implemented Generative AI use cases, including synthetic data generation, test data augmentation, and telecom scenario simulation, to improve model robustness and address data scarcity challenges. Leveraged GitHub Copilot to standardize coding practices, accelerate feature development, and support rapid prototyping of AI/ML solutions across distributed engineering teams. Engineered scalable feature stores and real-time data pipelines using SQL, NoSQL, Apache Spark, and Kafka to support low-latency inference and real-time decision-making systems. Established automated MLOps workflows using CI/CD pipelines, Docker, Kubernetes, and MLflow to enable reliable model deployment, version control, monitoring, and lifecycle management. Integrated AI solutions with cloud-native services on AWS, ensuring alignment with enterprise security, governance, and performance standards. Implemented graph-based machine learning models using graph analytics and Neo4j to analyze network topology, detect complex fraud patterns, and optimize routing and dependency relationships across telecom infrastructure. Environment: AI and ML, Deep Learning, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Generative AI, GitHub Copilot, SQL, NoSQL, Spark, Kafka, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, MLflow, AWS, Graph Analytics, Neo4j, OpenCV, ONNX.

results.title: Sai Krishna Sriram
highlights:
GenAI Engineer @ AT&T | Building Innovative AI Solutions
...
GenAI Engineer at [AT&T](https://www.linkedin.com/company/att)
...
I’m a multidisciplinary AI & Data professional with experience in architecting, building, and deploying end-to-end data and machine-learning solutions across industries—from telecom and IoT to defense and edge robotics. With an M.S. in Data Science from the University of Colorado Boulder, I blend data engineering, classical ML, and generative AI to turn raw data into actionable insights and compelling products. Whether I’m designing scalable ETL pipelines in Spark and Airflow, engineering cloud-native microservices in Python/Go (AWS, GCP, Azure), or orchestrating large language models with LangChain and RAG for real-time retrieval applications, my goal is the same: deliver reliable, high-impact systems that drive business value. I’ve built data warehouses and DBT models for financial reporting, implemented vector search and OCR-powered metadata layers in HPC environments, and fine-tuned LLMs using PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Hugging Face for both customer-facing chatbots and complex analytics. My career began at Qualcomm and Absolutelabs, where I optimized ML-based test-failure detection frameworks and developed robust APIs and Mule integrations that improved operational efficiency. As a Graduate Research Assistant at CU Boulder’s HIRO Lab, I pushed the boundaries of robotic manipulation with hybrid ML methods, earning human-like dexterity in challenging environments. In each role, I’ve championed best practices in MLOps—CI/CD, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana), and reproducibility (MLflow)—to ensure seamless production roll-outs. I thrive at the intersection of data science, engineering, and product delivery. Whether collaborating with UX designers to integrate AI into interactive front ends, working with sales teams to demo generative-AI proofs of concept, or presenting findings to C-suite stakeholders, I’m passionate about translating complexity into clarity. Let’s connect if you’re looking for a hands-on leader who can turn data and AI into your next competitive advantage.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
